---
title: "Metabolite Data Missingness"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "I:/Documents/PhD files/Kim/UPBEAT/Data Files")
```

```{r}
setwd("I:\\Science\\MS\\users\\students\\Dygas_Natalia\\Documents\\PhD files\\Kim\\UPBEAT\\Data Files")
metabol <- read.csv( "metabol_raw_data.csv")
metabol <- metabol[,-c(2,4,5,234:259,262,263)]
```


```{r}
summary(as.factor(metabol$Category))
```

In this markdown file, we will explore the missingness of the data.

```{r}
library(naniar)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r}
names(which(colSums(is.na(metabol[,3:230])) > 0))
metabol %>% select_if(~any(is.na(.))) %>% gg_miss_var()
```

```{r}
max(metabol$xxlvldlc_pct)
summary(metabol$xxlvldlc_pct)
summary(metabol$lvldlce_pct)
summary(metabol$lvldlc_pct)
summary(metabol$xlvldltg_pct)
```
